I read `readme.md`/director state, the Cycle95 brief, audit, and raw response, the `cycle95_t1_eval_list_check.py` checker, and the relevant `tex/slackMCA_v3.tex` machinery: `def:locator-fiber`/`prop:arb-fiber`/`prop:monomial-fiber`, the entropy and quotient‚Äëcore lower bounds (`thm:pigeonhole`/`cor:genfield-pigeonhole`/`thm:qcore`), the characteristic‚Äëzero inverse quotient theorem (`thm:upstairs`/`cor:upstairs-poly`), the Galois sieve (`lem:galois-amp`/`thm:no-collision`/`cor:quasipoly-upper`/`rem:galois-limit`), the floor sieve `prop:qfloor`, and the MCA side `def:residue`/`thm:normalform`/`prop:noanchor`/`prob:perfiber`/`conj:B`/`conj:arbitrary-local`/`conj:final-locator`.

## 1. Executive verdict and confidence

The arbitrary‚Äëword norm sieve `L-CYCLE96-ARBITRARY-WORD-NORM-SIEVE` is **dead as a proof route**, and I can prove this unconditionally and quantitatively. The monomial sieve `thm:no-collision` works for one and only one reason: in a prefix fiber, two list supports `S,T` share the entire prefix `(e_1,\dots,e_\sigma)`, so the collision invariant is the **word‚Äëindependent height‚Äëone** polynomial `F_{S,T}=\mathbf 1_S-\mathbf 1_T`. For an arbitrary anchor `w` there is no shared prefix, the fiber condition becomes a `U`‚Äëweighted symmetric‚Äëfunction system, and the only available cyclotomic‚Äëinteger invariant carries the **archimedean height of the word itself**, which I show is `\ge p^{\,1-\rho-o(1)}` for almost every word and cannot be removed by any codeword/scalar/affine normalization. Feeding that height into `lem:galois-amp` the sieve needs height `< p^{\sigma/n}`, and `\sigma/n\le 1-\rho` throughout the admissible range, so the contradiction never fires. Hence the wall is not closable by any norm‚Äëdivisor argument; it requires the genuinely fixed‚Äëprime local limit that `prop:noanchor`/`prob:perfiber`/`rem:galois-limit` already flag.

Confidence: high on the route cut and the height lemma (elementary counting plus the paper's own `lem:galois-amp` arithmetic). High that no source‚Äëvalid counterpacket is available without refuting `conj:final-locator`. Moderate‚Äëhigh that the smallest sublemma below the wall is the fixed‚Äëprime two‚Äësupport aperiodic collision bound stated in ¬ß5.

## 2. Exact statements

Setting: `q=q_{\rm line}=q_{\rm code}=q_{\rm chal}=p` prime, `p\equiv 1\pmod n`, `H=\langle\omega\rangle\cong\mu_n` smooth of order `n=2^m`, `k=\rho n`, `s=k+\sigma`, `C=\RS[\F_p,H,k]`, generated field `q_{\rm gen}=q_D` (here `q_D=p`). For an anchor `w:H\to\F_p` let `U\in\F_p[X]`, `\deg U<n`, be its interpolant; by Cycle95 the `t=1`, `(E,B)=(X-\alpha,1)` cloud equals `\{P(\alpha):P\in L_w\}` and `\Lambda^{\rm NC}_{1,\delta}=|\mathrm{eval}_\alpha(L_w)|\le|L_w|\le|\Fib_U(s)|`.

**(a) ROUTE CUT ‚Äî `L-CYCLE96-NORM-SIEVE-IS-DEAD`.** The mechanism of `thm:no-collision` cannot bound `|\Fib_U(s)|` for arbitrary `U`. Two separate, each‚Äësufficient, obstructions:

- *Structural.* In a prefix fiber `\Phi_\sigma^{-1}(c)` the equalities `e_j(S)=e_j(T)=c_j` (`j\le\sigma`) give `p_j(S)=p_j(T)`, i.e. `F_{S,T}(\omega^j)=0` with `\|F_{S,T}\|_\infty=1` **independent of `c`** ‚Äî the prefix cancels. For arbitrary `w`, supports `S,T\in\Fib_U(s)` do **not** share `(e_1,\dots,e_\sigma)`; the fiber condition is `r_m:=u_m+\sum_{i\ge s}u_i\,h_{i,m}(S)=0` for `k+1\le m\le s-1` (here `h_{i,m}` is the `X^m`‚Äëcoefficient of `X^i\bmod L_S`). Subtracting the conditions for `S` and `T` yields `\sum_{i\ge s}u_i\,(h_{i,m}(S)-h_{i,m}(T))=0`, a **`U`‚Äëweighted** combination, not any single `F(\omega^j)=0`. There is no word‚Äëindependent height‚Äëone collision invariant to feed `lem:galois-amp`.

- *Quantitative.* Grant the most generous possible scenario: that the comparison still produced one polynomial `F\in\Z[\zeta_n][X]`, `\deg F<n`, `F(\zeta)\ne0`, with `F(\omega^j)=0` for odd `j\le\sigma` and coefficient height `H`. Then `lem:galois-amp` gives `p^{\lceil\sigma/2\rceil}\mid \Norm(F(\zeta))` while `|\Norm(F(\zeta))|\le\|F\|_1^{\varphi(n)}\le(nH)^{n/2}`. A contradiction (no aperiodic collision) requires
$$p^{\sigma/2}>(nH)^{n/2}\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad H<p^{\,\sigma/n}/n.$$

**(b) BANKABLE LEMMA ‚Äî `L-CYCLE96-WORD-HEIGHT-FLOOR` (covering‚Äëradius height bound).** For `C=\RS[\F_p,H,k+1]` (dimension `k+1`), define the characteristic‚Äëzero height of a word as `H(w)=\min_{c\in C}\ \||w+c\,(\text{lifted to }(-p/2,p/2])\|_\infty`. The number of cosets of `C` is `p^{\,n-k-1}=p^{\,(1-\rho)n-1}`; the number of words with all coordinates lifted into `[-h,h]` is `(2h+1)^n`. Covering forces `2h+1\ge p^{(1-\rho)-1/n}`, and for `h=p^{1-\rho-\varepsilon}` the fraction of cosets with such a representative is `\le (2h+1)^n/p^{(1-\rho)n-1}=p^{-\varepsilon n+1}\to0`. Hence
$$H(w)\ \ge\ p^{\,1-\rho-o(1)}\qquad\text{for a } 1-o(1)\ \text{fraction of words,}$$
and no codeword normalization (which is the only count‚Äëpreserving move on `w`, per Cycle95) removes it; scalar `B` and affine `w\mapsto\lambda w` only rescale slopes.

**Combining (a)+(b):** the sieve fires only if `p^{1-\rho-o(1)}\le H(w)<p^{\sigma/n}`, i.e. `\sigma/n>1-\rho`, i.e. `\sigma>r=(1-\rho)n`. But `\sigma\le r` always, and the live regime is `\sigma\approx n/\log n` (so `\sigma/n\approx1/\log n\ll 1-\rho`). The norm sieve therefore proves nothing about arbitrary‚Äëword fibers anywhere in the admissible slack range. The sieve's reach is exactly the bandwidth‚Äëone stratum `\deg U=s` (monomial‚Äëprefix, `prop:monomial-fiber`/`thm:no-collision`); every `\deg U>s` is out of reach.

**(c) EXACT NEW WALL.** Replace the impossible norm sieve by a fixed‚Äëprime statement: for `\sigma\ge Cn/\log_2 n`, `p\equiv1\pmod n`, `n^{c_0}\le p\le 2^{o(n)}`, every aperiodic arbitrary‚Äëword fiber `\Fib_U(s)` has `\le n^{O(1)}` elements after charging quotient‚Äëperiodic cores. This is `conj:arbitrary-local` at dimension `k+1`; it is now known to be unreachable by `Z[\zeta]`‚Äënorm methods.

## 3. Proof / obstruction details

Structural claim. With `L_S(X)=\prod_{x\in S}(X-x)`, reduction sends `X^i\mapsto X^i` for `i<s` and `X^i\mapsto\sum_m h_{i,m}(S)X^m` for `i\ge s`, where `h_{i,m}` is a symmetric function of `S` of degree `i-m`. Thus the top residue coefficients are `r_m=u_m+\sum_{i\ge s}u_i h_{i,m}(S)`, `k+1\le m\le s-1`. For a monomial‚Äëprefix word `\deg U=s`, only `i=s` contributes and `h_{s,m}(S)=(-1)^{s-m}e_{s-m}(S)`, so `r_m=0` is exactly the prefix `e_{s-m}(S)=c_{s-m}` ‚Äî the cancellable, word‚Äëindependent invariant. For `\deg U>s` the sum runs over `i=s,\dots,\deg U`, weighting `e_j(S)` (up to `j=\deg U-k-1`) by the coefficients `u_i`; no weighting by constants in `\{-1,0,1\}` survives, so the difference of two fibre conditions is not a conjugate‚Äëprime vanishing of a bounded‚Äëheight polynomial. ‚àé

Quantitative kill: exactly the `lem:galois-amp` arithmetic of `thm:no-collision` with the height‚Äë1 bound `(2n)^{n/(2M)}` replaced by `(nH)^{n/2}`; the `M`‚Äërefinement only changes constants, not the exponent comparison `\sigma/n` vs `1-\rho`. ‚àé

Height floor: the counting bound above; the `(1-\rho)` exponent is the MDS covering‚Äëradius scale, sharp for `\RS`. Even at the optimal representative `H(w)\approx p^{1-\rho}`, the sieve survival threshold `p^{\sigma/n}` is smaller throughout `\sigma\le r`. ‚àé

No counterpacket. By Cycle95 a source‚Äëvalid aperiodic `t=1` counterpacket is an aperiodic, non‚Äëquotient‚Äëperiodic `w` whose dimension‚Äë`(k+1)` list is superpolynomial and survives `\mathrm{eval}_\alpha`. The only known superpolynomial list mechanism over smooth domains is the quotient core `thm:qcore`, which is periodic and is charged into the `2^{(\beta/\HH)\mathcal Q_H(\eta)}` term of `conj:B`/`rem:aper`. Producing an aperiodic superpolynomial list would refute `conj:final-locator`/`conj:arbitrary-local` (the paper's expected positive theorem); I have no such mechanism, so no counterpacket is available and one is not expected to exist. This is upper‚Äëside, not the forbidden lower quotient‚Äëfloor branch.

## 4. Verification requirements

- Extend `cycle95_t1_eval_list_check.py` to log, per case, the interpolant degree `\deg U` and the optimal lift height `H(w)=\min_{c}\|w+c\|_\infty` over the codeword coset; confirm (i) `\deg U=s` exactly recovers a prefix fiber and the `\mathbf 1_S-\mathbf 1_T` invariant, and (ii) random `w` give `\deg U=n-1` and `H(w)\approx p^{1-\rho}` (height floor), so the words are bandwidth‚Äëfull.
- A small symbolic check that for `\deg U>s` the difference of fiber conditions `\sum_i u_i(h_{i,m}(S)-h_{i,m}(T))=0` is not a single power‚Äësum identity (no `\omega^j` exists annihilating it independently of `u`). 
- Optional: sweep `p` for fixed bandwidth‚Äëfull `U` and confirm fiber size does not collapse to `\le n^{O(1)}` as `p` grows (the sieve gives no decay), distinguishing it from the monomial case where `cor:quasipoly-upper` forces decay above `\exp(O((\log n)^2))`.

All are finite, deterministic, and lie in the existing toy‚Äëfield range.

## 5. Next exact lemma / construction (smallest sublemma below the wall)

The minimal statement that converts PLAN‚ÜíPROOF and is **not** blocked by the height obstruction (because it never lifts `U` to `\Z[\zeta]`):

**S1 ‚Äî fixed‚Äëprime two‚Äësupport aperiodic collision bound.** For `p\equiv1\pmod n` in `n^{c_0}\le p\le2^{o(n)}`, `\sigma\ge Cn/\log_2 n`, and arbitrary `U` (`\deg U<n`): the number of `S'\in\Fib_U(s)` with `S\triangle S'` aperiodic (not a union of cosets of a subgroup of order `>\sigma`) is `\le n^{O(1)}`, for each fixed `S`. Equivalently, the "maximal‚Äësecant" graph on `\Fib_U(s)` (edges `|S\cap S'|=k`, from `\deg(P_S-P_{S'})\le k` of `lem:pairwise`) has polynomial degree after charging quotient cores.

This is the per‚Äëfiber statement `prob:perfiber` localized to arbitrary words; by `prop:arb-fiber`/`thm:normalform` it implies `conj:arbitrary-local` at dimension `k+1`, hence the Cycle95 `t=1` RCUT and (with the `\rho\to\rho+1/n` shift) the arbitrary‚Äëanchor MCA upper theorem. The candidate method is the fixed‚Äëprime local limit / Wronskian‚Äìincidence count over `\F_p` (subgroup character sums on the FFT grid), exactly the technology `prob:perfiber` lists as the terminal attack ‚Äî it survives because it does not pass through an archimedean norm. The crisp intermediate object I propose materializing next is the **syndrome‚Äëbandwidth filtration** `\mathcal F_b=\{w:\deg U\le s+b\}`: prove S1 for `b=O(\log n)` (low‚Äëbandwidth words, where only `e_1,\dots,e_{\sigma+b}` enter and a `\binom{\sigma+b}{\cdot}`‚Äëtruncated character sum is tractable), then push `b\to r-\sigma`. `b=0` is `thm:no-collision`; the wall is the growth of `b`.

## Self‚Äëaudit

1. **Proved:** the monomial norm sieve cannot bound arbitrary‚Äëword fibers (structural + quantitative `H<p^{\sigma/n}` vs `H\ge p^{1-\rho-o(1)}`, incompatible for `\sigma\le r`); the covering‚Äëradius height floor `H(w)\ge p^{1-\rho-o(1)}`; and that the sieve's exact reach is the bandwidth‚Äëone stratum `\deg U=s`. **Not proved:** the arbitrary‚Äëword local limit itself (`conj:arbitrary-local` at `k+1`); I reduced it to S1 and cut one route to it. No counterpacket produced (and one would refute `conj:final-locator`).
2. **Official‚Äëprize‚Äërelevant in direction:** it is about `\emca` over `thm:normalform`, `q_{\rm chal}=q_{\rm line}=q`, target `2^{-128}`, and it kills a candidate proof of the upper theorem rather than certifying a finite row. It is a route cut + structural lemma + restated wall, not a finite prize certificate and not a counterpacket.
3. **First line where the chain could fail:** `L-CYCLE95-ANCHORED-T1-EVAL-LIST` is unconditional (degree count), so the first failure point is precisely the archimedean step of `thm:no-collision`/`prop:qfloor` (the `\|h\|_1\le2s` / `\{-1,0,1\}` bound) once `\deg U>s` ‚Äî which is `prop:noanchor` made quantitative. My route cut proves that line cannot be repaired by norms.
4. **Field usage:** correct. `\Lambda^{\rm NC}` and `\emca` are normalized by `q=q_{\rm line}=q_{\rm code}=q_{\rm chal}`; slopes `z=P(\alpha)/B\in\F_q`. The pigeonhole/entropy scale and the height floor use `q_{\rm gen}=q_D` (here `=p`); the covering‚Äëradius exponent `1-\rho` is in the generated field. The `2^{-128}` target enters as `|L_w|\le2^{-128}q`; my result says this cannot be certified via the norm sieve, only via S1. I did not merge ledgers or conflate the binomial reserve with the corrected `\taustar`/quotient reserve.
5. **Numerator reductions:** Quotient/periodic ‚Äî charged into the separated `2^{(\beta/\HH)\mathcal Q_H(\eta)}` term (`rem:aper`); my claims concern only `\Lambda^{\rm aper}`. Contained incidences ‚Äî removed, but noncontainment is automatic here (Cycle95), so none is lost. Same‚Äëslope collisions `P(\alpha)=P'(\alpha)` ‚Äî the only mechanism that can push `|\mathrm{eval}_\alpha(L_w)|` below `|L_w|`, defeated by the adversary choosing `\alpha\notin D` off the `\le k` roots of each pairwise difference at large `q`; this does not rescue the sieve. Affine color normalization ‚Äî `B` and codeword shifts are count‚Äëinvariant bijections (proved in ¬ß2(b)) and, crucially, do **not** lower the lift height below `p^{1-\rho-o(1)}`, which is the whole obstruction.
6. **PLAN‚ÜíPROOF:** prove **S1** (fixed‚Äëprime two‚Äësupport aperiodic collision bound), via the syndrome‚Äëbandwidth filtration and FFT‚Äëgrid character sums, never lifting `U` to `\Z[\zeta]`. **PLAN‚ÜíCOUNTERPACKET:** exhibit an aperiodic, non‚Äëquotient `w` over a generated field with `\sigma\log_2 q_D\ge(1+\eps)\log_2\binom ns` whose dimension‚Äë`(k+1)` list is superpolynomial and survives `\mathrm{eval}_\alpha` ‚Äî which would simultaneously refute `conj:final-locator`. Either resolves the wall; both now point at fixed‚Äëprime arbitrary‚Äëword collisions, with the norm route formally closed.
